heading: Administration, protection, and promotion; franchises for hotel and lodge accommodations
label: 441c Administration, protection, and promotion; franchises for hotel and lodge accommodations
text: The administration, protection, and promotion of said Badlands National Park shall be exercised under the direction of the Secretary of the Interior by the National Park Service, subject to the provisions of the Act of , entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes,” as amended by the Act of (Forty-first United States Statutes at Large, page 732): , That in advance of the fulfillment of the conditions herein the Secretary of the Interior may grant franchises for hotel and for lodge accommodations under the provisions of this section.August 25, 1916June 2, 19201See References in Text note below.1Provided
notes: References in TextThe Act of , entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, as amended by the Act of (Forty-first United States Statutes at Large, page 732), referred to in text, is , , as amended by , . The act , known as the National Park Service Organic Act, enacted sections 1, 2, 3, and 4 of this title and provisions set out as a note under , National Park Service and Related Programs. Sections 1 to 4 of the Act were repealed and restated as , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and section 100101(a), chapter 1003, and sections 100751(a), 100752, 100753, and 102101 of Title 54 by , §§ 3, 4(a)(1), 7, , , 3260, 3272. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables. Change of Name“Park” substituted in text for “Monument” pursuant to , which is classified to and which redesignated Badlands National Monument as Badlands National Park.Pub. L. 95–625, § 611section 441e–1 of this title

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions of other officers, employees, and agencies of Department of the Interior, with certain exceptions, to Secretary of the Interior, with power to delegate, see Reorg.
